content:
## Closure Checks
Before closing a deposit account, confirm that every condition in account_exists, account_status_is_open, after_fee_remaining_balance_is_zero is satisfied. Do not treat a dormant, restricted, missing, or already closed account as eligible. The final balance test applies after any closure-related fee decision has been reflected, so an account is not ready for closure while money would remain or a shortfall would be created.

## Handling the Request
1. Identify the account the customer wants to close and verify that it is the account record intended for closure.
2. Review the account status before discussing completion timing.
3. Confirm whether the post-fee remaining balance will be zero. If it will not be zero, explain the remaining step needed before closure.
4. If the customer is comparing alternatives before closing, use "Checking Account Comparison Guide" or "Savings Account Comparison Guide" rather than reciting product terms from this page.

## Closure Tooling
Use the closure tool pattern close_bank_account_{suffix} only after the eligibility checks are complete. This is a WRITE action and requires unlock: True. Supply required arguments account_id. Optional arguments are reason, waive_early_closure_fee when the conversation supports them. See "Internal: Deposit Account Hidden Tool Docs" for the matching hidden tool document before unlock.
title: Internal: Deposit Account Closure Policy
